evidence.quoted_text: BER substrate accumulation in MBD4-LOF (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine / cytarabine triphosphate incorporation into BER repair patches stalls replication. MBD4-specific: rescue by WT-MBD4 but not catalytic-dead mutant.

BER substrate accumulation in MBD4-LOF (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine / cytarabine triphosphate incorporation into BER repair patches stalls replication. MBD4-specific: rescue by WT-MBD4 but not catalytic-dead mutant.

GDSC2 pharmacologic stratification, drug=ceralasertib (ATR inhibitor): MBD4 True-LOF cell lines (n=14) show increased sensitivity vs MBD4-WT (n=914) on the Methods-rule cohort (no somatic MBD4). LN_IC50: Δ=-0.732, p=0.0215, Cohen's d=-0.503. AUC and

Stress test #1 — MSI purge. Re-runs ceralasertib LN_IC50 with all MSI-H lines excluded from both arms (rationale: MBD4-LOF correlates with MSI-H; signal could be confounded by global MSI sensitivity to ATR). Result: signal STRENGTHENS without MSI-H l

Stress test #2 — TP53 stratification. Restricts both arms to TP53-mutant lines (rationale: TP53 status modifies ATR-pathway dependency; >95% of HGSOC is TP53-mutant). Result: effect size LARGEST in TP53-mutant subpopulation. LN_IC50: Δ=-1.069, p=0.00

Stress test #3 — Leave-one-out. Removes one MBD4-LOF line at a time and re-runs the primary endpoint (rationale: a single highly-sensitive line could drive the n=14 result). Result: 14/14 iterations remain significant. max p=0.0452, min p=0.0082, all

Stress test #4 — Lineage stratification (non-bowel). Splits the primary endpoint by bowel/non-bowel lineage (rationale: MSI-H bowel lines are over-represented in MBD4-LOF pool; effect could be bowel-specific artifact). Result: non-bowel cohort holds

Axis partner — adavosertib (MK-1775; WEE1 inhibitor), same replication-stress checkpoint axis as ATR. Direction-consistent with the ATR primary endpoint (negative Δ = more sensitive in MBD4-LOF) and similar effect magnitude (Cohen's d=-0.359), but p=

Falsification arm — PARP1 expression in MBD4-LOF lines. Tests the competing hypothesis that MBD4-LOF sensitivity is driven by PARP1 upregulation (PARP-trapping pathway). Result: PARP1 is NOT elevated in MBD4-LOF — Mann-Whitney U p=0.605, median LOF 6

Pan-cancer screens and expression–sensitivity correlates link PARP pathway activity or PARP1 levels to drug response in HRD/DDR contexts; those signals are not MBD4-specific. Separately, some preclinical work discusses BER load or RNF144A–PARP1 routi
